Mohsin's work focuses on applying computational algorithms , Deep Learning and bioinformatics approaches to extract biological insight from experimental and omics-scale data.
His current research centers on the impact of chronic stress on neuroimmune signaling, with particular emphasis on microglial priming and gut–brain axis dysfunction.

Lead Scientist and Head of Molecular Virology Lab. Dr. Muzafar jan is Working on intervention of HIV (T/F virus vs Chronic virus) with Immune system.
The occupancy and micro heterogeneity of glycans on HIV envelope has frustrated all and render 97% surface of it immune silent, also has impede the structural characterization But recent immune flexibility towards recognition of this glycan shield has changed the view and renew hope that a better understanding of the env trimer will translate into new vaccine candidates and more effective anti-retro viral therapy.

Our Molecular Virology laboratory has been awarded research funding exceeding **1 crore** by the **Anusandhan National Research Foundation** under the ARG programme.

This support reinforces our commitment to rigorous research and impactful scientific discovery. We sincerely acknowledge our dedicated team and valued collaborators—this milestone reflects collective effort, shared vision, and scientific excellence. We are excited to announce the launch of our new project!

Evolution of crown epitopes within the V2 and V3 loops of the HIV-1 envelope glycoprotein.

The 'crown' of the V3 loop is a major target for neutralizing antibodies. Our lab tracks the genetic drift and selective pressure on these epitopes in diverse clinical cohorts. We utilize bioinformatics tools and structural modeling to predict how these mutations affect antibody binding and viral entry efficiency.

The ‘crown’ of the V3 loop is a major target for neutralizing antibodies. Our lab tracks the genetic drift and selective pressure on these epitopes in diverse clinical cohorts. We utilize bioinformatics tools and structural modeling to predict how these mutations affect antibody binding and viral entry efficiency.
Molecular and Functional Analysis of HIV-1 Interaction with Mannose binding Lectin like proteins.

This project explores how HIV-1 interacts with MBL-like proteins on commensal bacteria. We are investigating if these interactions facilitate or hinder viral transmission across mucosal surfaces. Using protein-protein interaction assays and molecular docking, we aim to map specific binding sites between the HIV-1 gp120 and microbial lectins.

This project explores how HIV-1 interacts with MBL-like proteins on commensal bacteria. We are investigating if these interactions facilitate or hinder viral transmission across mucosal surfaces. Using protein-protein interaction assays and molecular docking, we aim to map specific binding sites between the HIV-1 gp120 and microbial lectins.
Generation of recombinant viruses expressing FLAG and HA tags using mega-primer PCR.

Tracking viral particles in real-time requires specific tagging. We have developed a high-efficiency mutagenesis protocol to insert HA/FLAG tags into the variable loops of gp120 without compromising viral fitness. This allows for complex imaging and neutralization assays to see how the virus responds to different immune pressures.
